stemHtml: In accordance with OPS regulations, regarding the first aid oxygen, the quantity of undiluted oxygen carried on board a pressurised aeroplane with 8 passengers operated at pressure altitude 27000 ft, shall be sufficient for at least:

options.a: 2 passengers for the remainder of the flight after cabin depressurisation when the cabin altitude exceeds 8000 ft.
options.b: 2 passengers for the remainder of the flight after cabin depressurisation when the cabin altitude only exceeds 14000 ft.
options.c: 1 passenger for the remainder of the flight after cabin depressurisation when the cabin altitude exceeds 8000 ft.
options.d: 1 passenger for the remainder of the flight after cabin depressurisation when the cabin altitude only exceeds 14000 ft.
